item.title: Takaichi’s victory in Japanese election means a billion dollar payday for Berkshire Hathaway
item.description: The Japanese market has rallied by 12% so far in 2026 as investors respond to Takaichi’s expansionary budget promises and the renewed weakness in the ...

item.title: ‘I’m not made of money’: My heating engineer didn’t fix my radiators on his first visit. Do I pay him a second time?
item.description: “He charged $180 for the second visit for his time, including $40 for the part.”

item.title: Here’s why Goldman Sachs is warning of more selling for stocks this week
item.description: Goldman Sachs analysts led by Gail Hafif says some big influential CTAs are ready to push the sell button on U.S. stocks, especially if the S&P 500 si...

item.title: Bad Bunny fails to produce stock market sizzle for two big European brands
item.description: In a market where sometimes tenuous links spell big moves for stocks, the Super Bowl halftime performance from Bad Bunny failed to produce coattails.

item.title: My husband, 73, wants to sell our $300K rental and buy an annuity. Is that wise?
item.description: “He believes the annuity will net $1,600 per month, the same as the rent from the house.”
